Ad Text:
Senior AI Engineer (Agents & Platform)
Melbourne | Hybrid | 3 office days 
Contract: 6months+ 
Daily Rate: $1000 flex

We’re looking for a Senior AI Engineer to sit at the intersection of platform and product — building real-world agentic experiences while shaping the platform that powers them.

This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys shipping agents end-to-end, while also improving the underlying platform to make it easier for other engineers to build with AI.

What you’ll be doing:
Design and ship full-stack agent experiences (chat UIs, streaming responses, tool visualisation)
Build and integrate Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers into LLM workflows
Develop secure, user-aware agents (OAuth2/OIDC, JWT, scoped tool access)
Improve agent reliability in production through observability, tracing, and evals
Contribute to the AI platform — making it easier for teams to adopt and scale agentic systems
(Nice to have) Design multi-agent systems (A2A, orchestration, delegation patterns)
Tech & Experience:
Strong experience building LLM-powered agents / applications
Frontend capability (React or similar) for building interactive AI experiences
Experience with tooling frameworks / protocols (e.g. MCP, LangChain, custom tool schemas)
Understanding of auth in distributed systems (OAuth2, OIDC, JWT)
Experience with observability & evals (e.g. LangSmith, OpenTelemetry)
Ability to operate in fast-moving, ambiguous environments
What sets you apart:
You make progress without a playbook
You know when to prototype vs productionise
You collaborate across engineering, platform, and product
You challenge what’s not working and adapt quickly
This is a super interesting role that will give your resume such a boost!  We are specifically looking for candidates with " Real World" experience in the above ideally working for another Tech company where you have been able to do this in a commercial environment. 

Please apply via SEEK. 
Due to volumes of applications, we can't accept phone calls or enquiries for this role.  Simply apply if you're interested and we will contact you if you're a match.

We are currently looking for an Azure Data Engineer to support strategic objectives by improving how the business uses data.

The role combines hands-on Azure data engineering, technical leadership, and vendor management. With deep expertise in Azure Data Factory (ADF), the role leads enterprise data integration across Azure Synapse Analytics - defining requirements, guiding vendor delivery, and building critical components of the data platform.

Skill set / Experience needed -

Minimum seven years' experience supporting data platforms and data warehouse/BI solutions (data engineering, integration, and dashboards).
Tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology, Information Systems, or a related field.
Strong stakeholder management skills, including prioritising work and managing SME and senior leader expectations.
Extensive hands‑on experience with Azure Data Factory (ADF).
Strong experience delivering data integration solutions on Azure Synapse Analytics.
Working knowledge of Microsoft Fabric (Pipelines, Dataflows Gen2, Lakehouse, Warehouse).
Proven experience managing external data engineering vendors and delivery outcomes.
Ability to write high-quality technical requirements and solution designs.
Strong SQL skills and solid understanding of data warehousing principles.
Experience with Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 and Azure Blob Storage.
Strong understanding of ETL/ELT, data lakes, Medallion architecture, and data warehousing principles (dimensional modelling, data quality, and governance).
Hands-on experience with BI and reporting tools (Power BI).
High self-motivation and ability to work hands-on and independently when required.
Current driver's licence and access to a vehicle.

Highly Desirable -

Python and/or PySpark for data transformation and validation.
Experience implementing CI/CD for data platforms (Azure DevOps / Git).
Demonstrable experience migrating SSIS workloads to Azure Synapse and/or Microsoft Fabric

This role is a permanent position and is in office 5 days a week.


The client site is not based near public transport, so you must also have your own mode of transport.

Job Title: Postdoctoral Researcher (A) – Next-Generation AI & Vision-Language Models
Ad Text:
Based at Australian Institute Machine Learning (AMIL), City East Campus
Full-time, fixed term contract 3 years
Salary Range: $102,024 - $109,307 per annum (plus 17% superannuation)


At Adelaide University, we create the opportunities you need to achieve your ambitions – because when you thrive, we thrive.

We are transforming education for contemporary learners and global citizens. Building on a proud legacy and shaped by bold ambition, it’s a place of excellence and equity, where our vibrant community of staff are united by our purpose to inspire Australia’s future change-makers and create a better tomorrow.

Work that matters

Join a funded research project at the forefront of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), developing innovative evaluation tools and training methods to enhance complex reasoning, decision-making, and adaptability in next-generation AI systems. Your work will address critical limitations in current AI approaches and contribute to impactful, real-world solutions across sectors such as healthcare, business, and education, while strengthening Australia’s leadership in AI.

Curious to learn more? Explore the position description/selection criteria above to discover more about this opportunity.

The team

Reporting to Associate Professor Qi Wu, this role sits within the Australian Institute for Machine Learning (AIML). You will work alongside experts in machine learning, computer vision, and natural language processing, contributing to cutting-edge research and partnering with academic and industry stakeholders to deliver innovative, high-impact outcomes.

Established in 2018 and based at Lot Fourteen in Adelaide, AIML is globally recognised for research excellence and real‑world impact, collaborating with leading industry and government partners to apply cutting‑edge discoveries to challenges in sectors such as defence, space, healthcare, infrastructure and agritech.

Visit the AU website to learn more about the Australian Institute for Machine Learning.

Our people

Our people are guided by purpose, curiosity and a commitment to lifelong learning. We embrace authenticity, innovation and collaboration, and harness diverse thinking in our pursuit of excellence.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced, research-intensive environment. You will engage with a diverse network of collaborators across disciplines, contributing to innovative projects that push the boundaries of AI while maintaining strong connections to practical applications.

Learn more about our people, what we stand for and what we offer at Careers at AU.

Experience

To join our community and thrive in this role, you will likely have the following skills and experience:

PhD in machine learning, AI, computer science or related field
Programming experience in Python, C++ or other relevant language and experience in deep neural networks (Pytorch, tensorflow).
Experience and demonstrable expert knowledge in one or more or the following areas: deep learning; graph neural networks and/or probabilistic graphical models; computer vision; Large Language Models.
A good publication record in top tier machine learning and/or computer vision conferences or journals, commensurate with experience and opportunity.
A strong work ethic, and the ability to work well independently, and as a member of a broader team

Our commitment to inclusion and diversity

We are committed to fostering a culture of inclusion where diversity is celebrated and everyone feels respected and valued. Adelaide University is an equal opportunity employer, committed to creating a safe, inclusive, and equitable workplace where everyone can thrive. We strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability, and people of all ages, genders, cultural backgrounds, sexual orientations, and gender identities. We are committed to supporting flexible working arrangements and providing reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process.

Launch your future with Adelaide University now.

Applying is simple. Simply click on the Apply Now button and upload:

your current CV
a cover letter that tells us why you’re excited about the role

The online application form will list the specific selection criteria that you need to address.

Submit your application by 11:30pm Thursday 23 April 2026

The University reserves the right to close this advertisement before the closing date if a suitable candidate is identified.

For further information about this opportunity, please contact (quoting reference number 492783):

Simone Patterson
Talent Acquisition Officer
+61 8 8302 1700| careers.adelaideuniversity@adelaide.edu.au

As Tech Lead, you will own the data architecture and technical vision across a significant portfolio of finance systems, including general ledger, accounts receivable, budgeting and forecasting, reconciliation, and finance data and analytics platforms.

You will drive real change by designing scalable, intelligent finance systems and leveraging AI, automation, and cloud-native capabilities to deliver platform efficiency and innovation at scale.

What you'll do
Lead end-to-end solution design and implementation of complex, enterprise-grade finance platforms
Optimise the use of core platforms including Fusion General Ledger, TM1, Workiva, and Anaplan
Drive AI and automation initiatives to improve platform performance and reduce manual overhead
Communicate complex technical concepts clearly to stakeholders across finance, technology, and executive audiences
Why this role?

Join this globally recognised financial services organisation with a strong technology culture and a genuine appetite for innovation. This is not a maintenance role; it's a platform for ambitious technologists who want to architect the future of finance systems at serious scale.

You'll work alongside high-calibre peers in a collaborative, intellectually curious environment with access to cutting-edge tools and the autonomy to make a real impact.

What you'll bring
Proven hands-on experience with big data and data integration at enterprise scale
Deep domain knowledge in finance, ideally gained within a major financial institution or consulting environment
Practical experience with finance platforms such as Fusion General Ledger, TM1, Workiva, or Anaplan
Experience working in a mature cloud environment and leading complex, multi-stakeholder implementation programmes
Strong communication and influencing skills, you can translate technical complexity into business value
Genuine passion for AI, automation, and using technology to solve real business problems
For more information please contact Matthew Nicholas at matthew@lansonpartners.com

About The Team

The College of Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics was formed in January 2019 from an amalgamation of the Schools of Veterinary and Life Sciences, Health Professions, Psychology and Exercise Science, Engineering and Information Technology, and Education. The combination of these individually strong areas will foster cross-disciplinary academic expertise and innovative multidisciplinary approaches to research and teaching. The College promotes excellent teaching and research facilities and has highly qualified academic and professional staff across the following disciplines:

Engineering and Energy
Information Technology, Math and Statistics
Chemistry and Physics

Your New Role!

This position provides support to senior researchers through assisting in undertaking research tasks within the research team. This includes the system design and implementation, collection of and log of experimental data, submission of statement reports, spreadsheet development, data interpretation, professional report writing.

Your Key responsibilities will include -

Research Support for Multimodal Sensing Systems: Contribute to the design, development, and experimental implementation of multimodal sensing systems (e.g., RGB and thermal imaging) for dairy cattle monitoring; Support research activities related to system configuration, data acquisition, calibration, and experimental validation in controlled and field environments.
AI System Integration and Implementation for Research Experiments: Assist with the implementation and optimisation of AI models for real-time behavioural analysis on edge computing platforms (e.g., Jetson-based systems); Support experimental evaluation of model performance, including processing efficiency, system robustness, and reliability under varying environmental conditions.
Experimental Field Trials and Data Collection: Participate in field-based research trials conducted in dairy farm; Support the collection, management, and quality assurance of research datasets, and contribute to experimental testing, troubleshooting, and documentation of system performance for research and reporting purposes.
Prototype Development for Research Platforms: Support the development and integration of prototype sensing platforms; Assist in evaluating hardware configurations, sensor integration approaches, and prototype improvements to support research objectives and system scalability.

Please refer to the Position Description for further details.

What We Offer You!

This Fixed Term (12 Months) | Full Time (37.5 hours per week) attracts a remuneration of $79,915 - $107,770 plus 17% employer superannuation contributions.

You'll join a dynamic and talented team where diversity and innovation thrive. Our Silver-tier status in the Australian Workplace Equality Index (AWEI), awarded in 2025, reflects our commitment to LGBTIQA+ inclusion, equity, and creating a safe, welcoming environment for all. We strive to be the university of choice for First Nations peoples, honouring Indigenous knowledge. Sustainability is central to our community and guided by clear, confident leadership, we are working toward an ambitious future with goals of net zero emissions and zero waste by 2030.

As well as the flexibility to maintain a work life balance, our staff enjoy a vibrant, natural campus with world-class facilities, along with a variety of benefits, including:

Enjoy a 2% salary increase under the Enterprise Agreement, effective July 2026
Flexible work arrangements
Cultural Load and allowance - providing up to $8,944 per year to First Nations employees who are contributing cultural knowledge and perspectives beyond the scope of their role
Indigenous Language Allowance
Salary packaging options - such as purchasing leave, gym membership, car parking permit
Employee Assistance Program
On-campus services and facilities, such as a Childcare Centre, Gym, Vet Hospital, and Chiropractic Clinic

For further information, check out our benefits and services page!

Join us!

Please apply online via the Apply Now button, along with submitting the following documents to support your application -

A cover letter including a statement addressing the selection criteria (with reference to the Position Description)
Your most recent resume (CV)

What you'll bring to the role:

Completion of a tertiary qualification in Engineering, Mechatronics, Robotics, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a closely related field.
Demonstrated experience in hardware integration, system installation, and configuration of sensing devices (e.g., cameras, thermal sensors, embedded systems).
Strong programming skills relevant to AI deployment and system integration, including Python, C/C++, Linux environments, and experience working with edge computing platforms (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson or similar).
Demonstrated experience in implementing and evaluating AI models in real-time or embedded environments, including performance optimisation and experimental validation.


Applicants should refer to the Position Description for further details on the work requirements, duties, skills and experience required for this role. Applications sent by post or email will not be accepted.

Please note visa sponsorship is not available for this position. Applicants are expected to have current and valid work rights in Australia.

Please see the Applicant Guide for more information. Applicants who have support or access requirements, are encouraged to advise this at the time of their application, to ensure appropriate assistance is provided throughout the recruitment process.

Contact Us

For enquiries about the opportunity on offer please reach out to Hai Wang, Associate Professor, and quote JR105138 as the reference.

Applications Close on 10th April 2026 (11:59pm AWST)

AI Engineer (Product-Focused)

Build AI into a real-world construction business having end nd-to-end ownership and focusing on shipping, iterating, and solving real problems. 

My client is a commercial construction company delivering high-end government and public infrastructure projects across NSW.

Over the past 12 months, the business has made a serious investment in AI - not just tools, but a custom-built AI ecosystem already embedded across tendering, contract admin, and project delivery.

We’re looking for an AI Engineer to take what’s been built and push it further.

This is a high-ownership, standalone role where you’ll:

Own and evolve the company’s AI ecosystem
Build real, production-ready AI solutions used daily by the business
Turn real-world problems into working systems
Help shape a future AI SaaS product for the construction industry
 What You’ll Actually Do
Design and build AI-powered workflows across:
Tendering & bids
Project delivery & reporting
Contract administration
Work heavily with LLMs (OpenAI, Claude, etc.) to create practical, embedded solutions
Develop and improve RAG systems and internal knowledge layers
Integrate AI into existing business systems (end-to-end, not in isolation)
Translate business problems into working AI products and automations
Continuously experiment, iterate, and improve what’s already live
Contribute to a commercial AI product being developed for the wider market
This isn’t about ticking every technical box but about how you think and build.

You’ll likely be someone who:

Has hands-on experience building and shipping AI applications (not just experimenting)
Is strong with LLMs, APIs, and AI tooling
Understands how to go from problem → solution → production
Has a product mindset, you care about usability, outcomes, and impact
Has high agency, you don’t wait for instructions, you figure things out
Is comfortable working in a lean, fast-moving environment
You might come from:

AI/ML Engineering
Product Engineering / Full-stack with AI focus
Startup / founder / Antler-style environments
Even Product or Data Science, if you’ve built real AI solutions
Bonus:

Side projects in AI
Experience deploying LLM/RAG systems into production
Interest in construction / real-world industries
If interested, please "apply now" or email a copy of your resume to 

shauna.oreilly@talentweb.com.au

About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

About the program:

AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.

About the role:

As a Systems Engineer, you’ll help shape next-generation capability by applying Model-Based Engineering (MBE) to design, develop and integrate complex systems. You’ll work closely with specialists, partner organisations and cross-functional teams to deliver solutions that strengthen Australia’s Defence and national security outcomes. You will:

Analyse and transform system and sub-system requirements into detailed designs within an MBE environment

Define and manage system interfaces, ensuring full traceability throughout the design lifecycle

Conduct trade studies and optimise design solutions against performance, cost, and schedule considerations

Produce and maintain technical artefacts including design descriptions, requirements documents, and analysis reports

Participate in design reviews and support project planning, stakeholder engagement, and engineering assurance activities

Contribute to estimates, bids, and continuous improvement initiatives across programs 




About You

You are a capable Systems Engineer who enjoys tackling complex technical problems and working in multidisciplinary teams. You will have:

Degree qualifications in a relevant Engineering Discipline or relevant industry experience

Knowledge and experience in the application of System Engineering, Integration and Verification on large complex systems

Good knowledge in one or more of Command & Control, Command Support or Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance domains in a Defence context

Experience with development of systems design for large complex systems

Understanding in the application of Model Based Engineering (MBE) principles

Sound knowledge and experience working within an Agile (Scrum) development project

An Australian NV-1 security clearance and ability to access/handle Export Controlled technology, data and information




Why join us?

At Lockheed Martin Australia, you’ll work on programs that push the boundaries of innovation, engineering excellence and mission capability. Whether you’re passionate about systems design, integration, digital engineering or model-based development, you’ll have opportunities to learn, lead and grow.

Be part of something bigger — help shape the future of Defence systems and advanced technology.

How we reward and support you: 

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

A variety of work patterns

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs

Special Requirements

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

Equal Opportunity Statement

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees' unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

Job Title: Senior AI Engineer - Investment & Decision-making products
Ad Text:
Senior AI Engineer - Product Engineer
Global Investment & Strategy Business
Sydney | Hybrid
  
We’re genuinely excited to be partnering with a leading global investment firm as they continue to build out their internal AI capabilities.
  
This is a rare opportunity to join a highly respected, well-capitalised business that is taking a thoughtful, long-term approach to applying AI in real-world decision-making — not experimentation for its own sake, but tooling that directly impacts how capital and investment decisions are deployed.
  
The Role
We’re looking for a Product-Oriented AI Engineer to design and build internal systems that transform how investment teams operate. AI is already being embedded into workflows across research, analysis and decision-making. Your role will be to take this further — identifying opportunities, building solutions and delivering production-grade products that are actually used. This is a hands-on engineering role with strong product ownership. You’ll be expected to both build and shape what gets built.
  
What You’ll Do

Build and evolve internal products and platforms used in real investment workflows
Own the end-to-end lifecycle — from discovery and prototyping through to production and iteration
Design and implement AI/LLM-enabled systems, agents and data workflows
Turn early-stage ideas and experiments into scalable, durable products
Work closely with end users to ensure what you build is practical, adopted and high impact
Integrate data sources, APIs and tools into clean, cohesive systems
Operate in fast iteration cycles, balancing speed with quality and reliability
Contribute to both product direction and technical architecture
What Success Looks Like

Tools you build become core to day-to-day workflows
Measurable improvements in efficiency, insight and decision-making
Reduction in manual, repetitive work through shared internal capabilities
Products that are designed to scale across teams and use cases
Increasing influence over both what gets built and how it’s delivered
About You

4+ years’ experience as a software engineer in a fast-paced environment
Strong track record of shipping end-to-end products
Comfortable working across the stack (e.g. Python, APIs, cloud, data systems)
Experience with or strong interest in AI/LLMs, automation or data-driven systems
Product mindset — you think in terms of user problems and outcomes, not just code
Comfortable with ambiguity and evolving problem spaces
High ownership and accountability for what you build
Familiar with modern tooling, including AI-assisted development
Why This Role

Work on real AI applications embedded in critical workflows
High ownership and autonomy from day one
Direct exposure to investment decision-making at a global level
Backed by a business with serious scale, ambition and long-term thinking
A rare blend of engineering depth and product influence
Culture & Benefits - loads of above beyond market perks and benefits with additional annual and parental leave etc
  
This is a high-impact role with real ownership, strong engineering depth and meaningful product influence. If it sounds like you, apply or get in touch to learn more.

Company Description


Forward with Cuscal 

At Cuscal, we’re not just shaping the future of payments - we’re pioneering it. From launching Australia’s first ATM to being the first certified NPP PayTo Payer and Initiator in 2022, we’ve spent over 59 years building solutions that millions of Australians rely on every day.  

Now, we’re preparing for what’s next, and we want you to be part of it. 

Join us as an Environment Automation Engineer in our Engineering Practice team and help us deliver ground-breaking solutions that push the boundaries of what’s possible. 

Job Description


The Environment Automation Engineer is responsible for designing and delivering automated environment management solutions that support development, testing, and production teams. Operating within the Engineering Practices function, the role enables squads and domains to efficiently provision, manage, and maintain secure, reliable environments aligned with organisational standards.

The role focuses on reducing manual effort through automation, optimising tools and workflows, and promoting self‑service and operational resilience. In addition, the Environment Automation Engineer provides hands‑on support, guidance, and capability uplift to teams, enabling sustainable, high‑quality environment management practices and long‑term operational excellence.

Qualifications


What you’ll bring 

To succeed in this role, you’ll have:

Experience with CI/CD tools such as GitHub & GITHUB Actions
Experience with Scripting (Bash, PowerShell, Python) for automation
Experience with Terraform and Infrastructure‑as‑Code in AWS
knowledge of End-to-End system design & integrations
Knowledge of SLDC and various phases of Testing
Working knowledge of YAML, JSON, and XML
Versatile to explore, build POC and deliver new Solutions using technologies and Tools
Hands on experience in delivering solutions
Defining and building automation patterns
Supporting developers in design and build
Supporting testers in coverage and execution
Resolving roadblocks
Problem-solving skills
Identifying key delivery risks
Sequencing of tasks

You will stand out from the crowd with the following:

Solid understanding of networking (DNS, TCP/IP, load balancing) and security fundamental
Database experience (SQL/Oracle)
Knowledge for Environment Management
Knowledge on Control-M Development
Experience with tools such as Splunk, CloudWatch, or BMC for tracking system performance, troubleshooting and the creation of dashboards for actionable insights.

Additional Information


Why Cuscal? 

At Cuscal, you’ll find a strong, successful company that’s reimagining the future. And our team is right there at the heart of it all.   

Here, you’ll deliver or support interesting, ground-breaking work that has real impact - on Australia’s financial services sector and the millions of customers it serves. You’ll innovate alongside skilled, smart, connected teams. And you’ll build an impressive, fulfilling career that continues to grow.   

You’ll also enjoy a range of benefits, including: 

Work in a hybrid model that supports your lifestyle and goals.
We celebrate success: Our IGNITE program recognises individual and team achievements.
Wellbeing focus: We support your physical, mental, and financial health with holistic initiatives and access to discounts via ‘Cuscal Advantage.

Join us and reimagine the future 

If this role excites you, we’d love to explore your potential and vision for the future. Screening and interviews may occur before the job ad closing date, so don’t wait - apply now. 

For further information about this role please contact Careers@cuscal.com.au  

Cuscal is an equal opportunity employer committed to an equitable, diverse, and socially inclusive work environment and a positive, barrier-free recruitment process.  

We welcome applicants from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage, people living with a disability, LGBTQIA+ and people from culturally diverse backgrounds to explore career opportunities with Cuscal.   

Note: Cuscal does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruitment agencies or search firms.

About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

About the program:

AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.


About the role:

We’re seeking a Support System Architecture Team Lead to join the AIR6500 Operational Continuity team. In this role, you’ll lead a team of engineers while shaping how support systems are designed and delivered for one of Australia’s most advanced Defence programs.

This is a unique opportunity to define and lead the Support System architecture for AIR6500 by combining systems engineering, operational continuity, and sustainment to ensure the system is reliable, supportable and mission-ready from day one.

You’ll play a key role in designing how the system is supported across its lifecycle, working across engineering, logistics, ICT/cyber and sustainment teams to deliver a cohesive, fit-for-purpose Support System.

Key responsibilities

Lead the design and development of the AIR6500 Support System architecture ensuring alignment with operational continuity and sustainment needs

Manage and mentor a team of engineers, setting clear technical direction and priorities

Apply systems engineering principles across the full lifecycle

Collaborate across multidisciplinary teams including engineering, ILS, ICT, cyber, infrastructure and sustainment

Ownership to ensure the design meets supportability, reliability (RAM), safety, security and environmental requirements

Support design reviews, resolve technical challenges and maintain end-to-end design integrity

 

About you:

You’re an experienced Systems Engineer or Architect with a background in Operational Continuity, Support Systems or ILS and you enjoy leading teams while solving complex system-level challenges.

You bring a strong mix of technical depth and leadership, and are comfortable working across multiple disciplines to shape practical, long-term support solutions.

Essential skills and experience:

Strong experience in systems architecture and systems engineering lifecycle

Background in Operational Continuity, Support Systems or Integrated Logistics Support (ILS)

Experience leading or mentoring engineers and coordinating technical work

Ability to work across multi-disciplinary teams and complex stakeholder environments

Experience ensuring design integrity, integration and technical assurance across complex systems

Engineering degree (Systems, Electrical, Mechanical, Aerospace or similar)

Job Description

The team

At SEEK, our AI Platform Services (AIPS) teams build platforms and solutions that solve complex information problems and enable AI innovation across the SEEK Group. Teams are cross-functional, multi-disciplinary squads that work within an APAC matrix organisational structure. The AI Platform Engineering teams have a specific mission to provide shared infrastructure, governance, and reusable assets that empower AI and product teams to experiment safely, ship faster, and continuously deliver intelligent, differentiated experiences at scale.

You will work with roles across the engineering spectrum from graduate to lead level, including Software Engineers, Engineering Managers, Heads of Engineering, Software Architects, Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Platform Engineers, Product Managers, Business Analysts, and Iteration Managers.

About the role 

A Staff Platform Engineer is a technical lead and senior member within our AI Platform Services (AIPS) Engineering teams. You'll be responsible for the technical strategy and direction of SEEK's GenAI and ML Platforms, ensuring these systems are highly available, resilient, and enable AI innovation at scale. Embedded within a team, you'll lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS and SEEK more broadly. Your primary focus is designing and building AI platform solutions that enable Data Scientists, Engineers, and product teams to deliver AI-powered experiences rapidly and safely. You bring in-depth technical understanding of AI platform infrastructure, multi-cloud architectures (AWS, GCP), and the operational requirements of GenAI and ML systems, while driving alignment of your team's technical roadmap to SEEK's overall AI strategy.

Responsibilities 

Design and evolve technical architecture for GenAI and ML platform systems, ensuring they are well-architected, highly available, and aligned with SEEK's development practices and technical metrics
Drive technical strategy that enables SEEK's AI innovation at scale, leading architecture decisions for multi-cloud AI infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and aligning platform capabilities with business objectives
Build and operate highly scalable, performant AI infrastructure using AWS and Python for platform automation, tooling, and integrations while exemplifying clean, well-tested, secure code
Lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS, serving as the key point of contact for technical solutions and system interfaces
Partner with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to enable self-service model development, deployment, and operation while strengthening engineering discipline through understanding of AI/ML operational requirements
Support the Engineering Manager in capacity planning, risk management, and scoping of technical work while building strong relationships with Architecture, Security, and other AI platform teams
Grow team member technical capabilities through coaching on AI/ML platform patterns and practices while fostering a culture of safety, inclusivity, and diversity
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Proven experience as a Staff Engineer, Principal Engineer, or similar technical leadership role building and operating production platform systems at scale
Strong expertise in cloud infrastructure and platform engineering, with deep hands-on experience in AWS services and multi-cloud architectures
Advanced Python programming skills with demonstrated experience building platform automation, tooling, and integrations for highly scalable systems
Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform), container orchestration (Kubernetes), and observability tools for platform operations
Track record of leading technical roadmaps and driving platform health initiatives aligned with key technical metrics and SRE principles
Experience building platforms that enable self-service capabilities for engineering and data teams, with understanding of developer experience and platform adoption
Hands-on MLOps or ML Engineering experience, GenAI/LLM platform knowledge (model inference, vector databases, RAG patterns), GCP/Vertex AI experience, or ML lifecycle tooling expertise are good to have, but not a must.
